qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection in generated MCP server via unescaped operation summary
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable component (@orval/mcp), same root cause (unescaped summary field interpolation in MCP server generation at packages/mcp/src/index.ts:209), same injection mechanism (malicious OpenAPI summary breaks out of string literal), and same CWE-77 code injection class as described in advisory GHSA-mwr6-3gp8-9jmj.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection in generated TypeScript via unescaped default values in stringify()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component (@orval/core vs @orval/mcp), different injection sink (stringify() for parameter default values at packages/core/src/utils/string.ts:19-21 vs summary field interpolation at packages/mcp/src/index.ts:209), different code path. While related in class, this is a distinct vulnerability not described by the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Code injection in generated MCP server via unescaped operation summary

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes code injection in @orval/mcp via unescaped summary field interpolation. The finding identifies the exact same root cause at packages/mcp/src/index.ts:209 where verbOption.summary is interpolated into generated server.ts without escaping. The PoC was executed: orval was built from source at the pinned vulnerable commit b8189efe3, a malicious OpenAPI spec with an injected summary was processed, and the generated server.ts contained arbitrary require('child_process').execSync() code. The injected payload was extracted and executed, creating /tmp/evohunt_mcp_proof as proof of arbitrary code execution. This is end-to-end exploit success matching the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Vulnerable code confirmed at packages/mcp/src/index.ts:209: '${verbOption.summary}' interpolated without escaping
Generated server.ts line 31 contains injected code: ''); require('child_process').execSync('echo EVOHUNT_MCP_INJECTION_SUCCESS > /tmp/evohunt_mcp_proof && echo MCP_INJECTED'); //'
Injected payload executed successfully, /tmp/evohunt_mcp_proof created with content 'EVOHUNT_MCP_INJECTION_SUCCESS'
Root cause matches advisory: MCP server generation logic incorporates summary field from OpenAPI spec without validation or escaping

poc_executions.observed_effect: Orval processed malicious OpenAPI spec and generated server.ts containing injected require('child_process').execSync() code at line 31. The injected payload was extracted and executed, creating /tmp/evohunt_mcp_proof proving arbitrary code execution via the advisory-described vulnerability.
